search_queries_used: site:academic.oup.com/bjd vitiligo guideline 2021 full history site type extent stability photography
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic thyroid testing, medicine, phototherapy, dose, referral, or treatment interval
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The guideline explicitly does not cover diagnosis.
All investigations and management remain clinician-entered.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults or children with clinician-suspected or established vitiligo; rapid progression, diagnostic uncertainty, autoimmune disease, thyroid, pregnancy, child, and psychosocial context need qualified review.
setting_applicability: UAE primary and specialist dermatology assessment.
UAE_applicability: History, impact, and photography are transferable, but UAE diagnosis, laboratory, medicine, phototherapy, psychology, pediatric, and referral pathways remain local.
recency_verification: The BAD vitiligo guideline published in 2022 and titled 2021 was reviewed on 2026-07-14; its planned 2026 revision has not yet been identified as published.
superseded_check: No newer published BAD vitiligo guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact history and impact section supports documenting a vitiligo context, onset, extent, stability or progression, triggers, function, quality-of-life and psychosocial impact, concerns, clinician skin inspection and distribution, and prior records without diagnosing vitiligo.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting lesion site and distribution, clinician-established type, extent, stability, speed, triggers, quality-of-life and psychosocial impact, and personal or family autoimmune and thyroid context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact monitoring section supports documenting clinical photographs reviewed when available and consented without inventing progression or response.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports recording medical photography and documented affected areas for clinician-led monitoring without creating a response conclusion or fixed interval.

unresolved_source_gaps: Exact type, body-surface extent, stability, progression rate, triggers, autoimmune and thyroid history, psychosocial assessment, diagnosis, laboratory testing, treatment, medicine, phototherapy, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
